message:
Here's a wild one: your dog can smell in STEREO. 🐶👃 Each nostril works independently, sniffing the air on its own, so their brain can compare the two and figure out exactly which direction a scent is coming from — kind of like how our two eyes build one 3D picture. That's why a tracking dog weaves side to side with its nose down: it's literally steering by smell. Oh, and they tend to lead with the right nostril for brand-new scents! 🧭

Tag someone whose dog can sniff out a treat from across the house! 🍖
